title: Heart of Graush
content:

{{Quote|The dead king Dathka Graush replaced his own heart with this fist-sized ruby, which contained the souls of his foes.|[[Sorzus Syn]]|<ref name="BoS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>}}
Through the use of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] and [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemy]], the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[King/Legends|King]] [[Dathka Graush]] replaced his own [[Heart/Legends|heart]] with a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitive]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] later called the '''Heart of Graush'''. The Heart of Graush was made of the same material as the [[Great crystal|crystals]] within Sith crypts that housed the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirits]] of vanquished [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]]. Within the heart were the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] spirits of thousands of long-dead Sith. Graush used the Heart to make himself almost invincible.

Following the [[Discovery of Korriban|arrival]] of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Exiles]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] in [[6900 BBY]],<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> the ''[[Jen'jidai]]'' [[Sorzus Syn]] took an interest in studying a multiplicity of preserved [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]], recording her notes on the subject in [[Sorzus Syn's chronicle|her chronicle]]. She described the Heart of Graush as one of the most powerful amulets she had encountered during this time, leading her to include a sketch of the crystal and an accompanying discussion of its design and purpose in her chronicle.<ref name="BoS" />

[[1 BBY/Legends|Thousands of years]] after the Graush's [[Death/Legends|death]], the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] vessel ''[[Jynni's Virtue]]'', [[Naz Felyood]], discovered the Heart buried in a [[Dathka Graush's tomb|tomb]] in the [[Valley of Golg]]. This led to the Captain being possessed by Graush. In the end, Felyood's comrades destroyed ''Jynni's Virtue'', and Felyood lost the Heart in the process. He wandered [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] for a long [[Time/Legends|time]] afterward, ageless, constantly searching for the Heart.
